unchangedJobs.title: R&D electrical engineer
unchangedJobs.description: The Opporutnity As new R&D Electrical Engineer, you will join our Global Multi-Physic Simulation (GLOMUS) network team in the Technology Center of HITACHI Energy. You will be part of a group of key people with specific expertise in the fields of electrical, structural, acoustic, thermo-mechanic and fluid-dynamics analyses, simulation and testing. As part of our global R&D community including Technology Centers and Research Centers worldwide, you will contribute to support all factories in the PG Dry, Traction and Distribution Transformers on innovative and challenging research projects. You will focus on developing new technologies and products, optimizing existing ones and on supporting different engineering offices around the world. How you will make an impact Study of current state of art in the field of 2D and 3D simulation of transformers Analysis of performance of different parameters of dry transformers by means of 2D and 3D electromagnetic FEM calculations Develop new automations for the assessment of products through electromagnetic simulations coupled with structural, thermomechanical and acoustic simulations Run feasibility studies, develop new technical concepts, design new products and simulate their expected performances Organize prototyping and testing activities to validate new technologies\/products Lead product and technology development projects in the field of Dry, Traction and Oil Distribution Transformer, delivering results on time and on budget at agreed scope and quality Support the transfer of technology and knowledge from R&D to engineering department by writing design rules, preparing trainings and collaborating with design tools team Provide technical support to other departments when needed (engineering, product management, marketing and sales etc.) Living Hitachi Energy's core values of safety and integrity, which means taking responsibility for your own actions while caring for your colleagues and the business. Your background MSc + 1 years of experience in Electrical Engineering field PhD in Electrical Engineering is preferred Existing work experience in Electrical Engineering with particular focus on transformer design Background experience on acoustics of transformers. Advanced knowledge of transformer design and prediction of relevant performance such as leakage fields, stray losses, no-load losses, short-circuit forces, mutual inductances, and similar Experience in the finite element analysis (ANSYS, COMSOL, FEM electromagnetic simulation tool) Knowledge of at least one of the following programming languages ( Python, Matlab, C#) Experience in electric testing of transformers Project management skills Action and result oriented mindset Innovation spirit, "out of the box" thinking More about us We pride ourselves on offering a holistic range of competitive benefit programs to support your financial, physical and mental wellbeing and personal development. We want you to truly thrive with us – in work and out. Our employee benefit programs are tailored to each country, depending on location of employment and job grade. During the recruitment process you will be given more specific information on the employee benefit program offering in your intended country of employment.

unchangedJobs.title: Production Operator 80 - 100% (f/m/d)
unchangedJobs.description: The opportunity In this role, you will be an essential part of our production team within the electromechanical and transformer environment. You will perform assembly and production tasks along the manufacturing line and contribute to high product quality and efficient processes. Working in a structured environment, you will play a key role in ensuring smooth operations and continuous improvement. How you will make an impact Assemble electromechanical components using hand and power tools Perform manual tasks and production line activities according to work instructions Read and interpret technical drawings, specifications, and assembly instructions Conduct quality inspections on semi-finished and finished products and identify deviations Document and report discrepancies and quality issues Follow all HSE (Health, Safety, and Environment) guidelines and report non-compliance Maintain a clean, safe, and organized workspace Collaborate with team members and support the training of new employees Your background Previous experience in industrial production, ideally in an electromechanical environment Good manual skills with a precise and reliable working style Basic ability to read and understand technical drawings and instructions Basic knowledge of Italian or English Willingness to work in shifts (if required) Team-oriented mindset with a strong sense of responsibility and safety awareness About us We are a global technology leader in the field of energy and electrification, committed to advancing a more sustainable and efficient future. Our solutions support the reliable transmission and distribution of electricity, helping industries and communities reduce their environmental impact. We offer a collaborative and inclusive working environment where safety, quality, and continuous improvement are at the core of everything we do. With opportunities for professional development and growth, we empower our employees to contribute their ideas and make a real impact every day.

unchangedJobs.title: Electrical Design Engineer - Dry Transformers 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
unchangedJobs.description: The opportunity Joining our Dry Transformer engineering team, you'll help shape next generation designs that improve safety, efficiency, and sustainability in critical power applications. You'll collaborate closely with R&D, production, quality, and supply chain to translate customer requirements into manufacturable solutions and see them through prototype, type testing, and serial release. Working across global projects and standards, you'll deepen your expertise in insulation systems, electromagnetic design, and thermal performance while contributing to the digitalization of our design processes. This is a high impact role where your work directly enables reliable electricity for industries and communities worldwide. How you'll make an impact Lead the development of design specifications and models, ensuring they meet all relevant standards and regulations Conduct thorough design reviews and verifications, implementing modifications to elevate project quality Monitor and manage project timelines, ensuring design milestones are achieved and projects are delivered on schedule Conduct risk assessments, develop mitigation strategies, and seek opportunities for design innovations Engage with customers to capture and incorporate their feedback into design solutions, enhancing customer satisfaction Collaborate with the supply chain team to ensure designs are manufacturable and cost-effective, selecting optimal materials and components Prepare and maintain detailed documentation that supports design processes and facilitates knowledge sharing Identify and implement continuous design improvements based on test results and industry best practices, enhancing quality and efficiency Your background Degree in electrical engineering or a related field 2-5 years of experience in electrical design engineering of dry type transformers Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills Ability to work both independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team English is a must, Italian is beneficial Pro-active attitude, self-motivated and with a strong sense of ownership Open to change, feedback and continuous improvement. More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

unchangedJobs.title: Environmental and Employee Health & Safety Specialist 80 – 100% (f/m/d)
unchangedJobs.description: The opportunity As a Factory Environmental and Employee Health & Safety Specialist part of the local HSE team, you will play a key role in participating to HSE development, with main focus on Environmental topic, but granting a good support on Health & Safety activities. Key tasks on the Environmental side will be related to safeguarding our site's environmental integrity and driving our sustainability ambitions forward. In this role, you will ensure full compliance with all relevant environmental laws, regulations, and permits, while fostering strong relationships with community stakeholders and environmental authorities. You will develop, implement, and maintain robust environmental policies and procedures that promote accountability across the organization. By conducting environmental risk assessments, delivering training on sustainability practices, and leading initiatives that improve resource efficiency, you will directly contribute to reducing the factory's environmental footprint and supporting long-term operational excellence. On the H&S part, you'll be required to support main Health and Safety development activities organized by the HSE Manager, delivering results according to Management expectations. Please submit your application in English. How you'll make an impact Ensure regulatory compliance with Swiss environmental law and related frameworks (USG\/EPA, LRV\/OAPC, GSchG\/WPA, VVEA, ChemA\/ChemO), including permit management and coordination with cantonal authorities (BAFU) Lead environmental impact assessments for new production lines, plant changes, and modifications, ensuring alignment with internal environmental policies Manage waste and disposals, including segregation, traceability, registers, supplier audits, and all special waste streams, while supporting wastereduction and VVEA improvement initiatives Oversee chemical compliance, including ChemA, ChemO, ORRChem, CLP, Manage and maintain Safety SDS requirements, and notifications of new substances Support safe handling and storage of dangerous goods (DG) by coordinating with relevant departments and ensuring ongoing compliance Plan and conduct environmental audits and inspections, identifying improvement areas and ensuring timely corrective actions, also providing environmental compliance support and training to Engineering, Maintenance, and overall Business Unit, including REACH\/RoHS documentation and sustainability-related data needs On the Health and Safety part, you're required to support HSE manager in H&S-related topics managing production activities and equipment with the highest safety standards, both on the practical part and on documentation preparation. Support HSE reporting activities required by governmental or Hitachi Energy internal stakeholders. Your background Min. 3 years of experience in environmental roles within the industrial sector and strong knowledge of Swiss environmental regulations and compliance obligations Solid understanding of best practices in emissions, waste, water, chemicals, and sustainability Strong analytical skills, including environmental data processing and use of relevant software tools Basic knowledge of H&S principles and concepts Excellent command of Office 365 applications Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to assess environmental risks and develop effective mitigation strategies Excellent verbal and written communication skills for engaging with diverse internal and external stakeholders Fluent in Italian and English More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.

Your background Min. 3 years of experience in environmental roles within the industrial sector and strong knowledge of Swiss environmental regulations and compliance obligations Solid understanding of best practices in emissions, waste, water, chemicals, and sustainability Strong analytical skills, including environmental data processing and use of relevant software tools Basic knowledge of H&S principles and concepts Excellent command of Office 365 applications Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to assess environmental risks and develop effective mitigation strategies Excellent verbal and written communication skills for engaging with diverse internal and external stakeholders Fluent in Italian and English More about us Our flexible working practices help you optimize personal and business performance while creating an environment where all employees can develop their skills and grow.
descriptionByLocale.en: The opportunity As a Factory Environmental and Employee Health & Safety Specialist part of the local HSE team, you will play a key role in participating to HSE development, with main focus on Environmental topic, but granting a good support on Health & Safety activities. Key tasks on the Environmental side will be related to safeguarding our site's environmental integrity and driving our sustainability ambitions forward. In this role, you will ensure full compliance with all relevant environmental laws, regulations, and permits, while fostering strong relationships with community stakeholders and environmental authorities. You will develop, implement, and maintain robust environmental policies and procedures that promote accountability across the organization.
